name: Hoagy Carmichael
description: American composer, pianist, singer, actor and bandleader (1899–1981)
abstract: Hoagland Howard Carmichael (November 22, 1899 – December 27, 1981) was an American musician, composer, songwriter, actor, author and lawyer. Carmichael was one of the most successful Tin Pan Alley songwriters of the 1930s and 1940s, and was among the first singer-songwriters in the age of mass media to use new communication technologies …

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: The Stardust Road: A Hoagy Carmichael Jubilee

works_created.t: In the Cool, Cool, Cool of the Evening
I Get Along Without You Very Well

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: Hoagy and I used to be very close. ... I did a lot of his early things with him. I mean arranging. ... Hoagy said he was going to come along with the band for a little while and we could work together

about_quotes.speaker: John Abercrombie
about_quotes.evidence: We made one CD, which was the music of Hoagy Carmichael’s, it was Hoagy Carmichael’s childrens’ music... Hoagy kind of hooked us up with his father. His father had this book of kid’s tunes... And we recorded them for Ahmad Jamal’s record label
about_quotes.doc_title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny Wiggs
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Hoagy Carmichael in the context of Sidney Ayrton ('A fellow who rode up the Lazy River with Hoagy Carmichael'). The interviewee does not mention Hoagy Carmichael or claim any personal interaction with him.

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] With Hoagy Carmichael and his orchestra. [INTERVIEWEE] Hoagy Carmichael. ... [INTERVIEWER] With Carmichael and one of the things you played was 'Stardust'. [INTERVIEWEE] Yeah, that's right. I did that.

about_quotes.speaker: Larry Ridley
about_quotes.evidence: my Uncle Ben... befriended Hoagy Carmichael. In fact my Uncle Ben always told me that the major part of the song “Stardust” was his. And Hoagy was very close to my uncle for all of his life.
about_quotes.doc_title: Larry Ridley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 10, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: I loved Hoagie... I wish I had had the chance to do an album of him... my brother-in-law’s widow is married to Hoagy Carmichael’s stepson. Clear as mud, right? But so we share that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998
